purpose: Machine-readable session admission gate. A llama-server session must be admitted before any campaign measurement counts.

warmup_procedure.step_1: After server reports healthy (/health returns ok), run 3 throwaway generic inference calls (discarded). Flushes CUDA initialization.
warmup_procedure.step_1b: Run 1 throwaway round of all 5 anchor probes (results discarded). Primes KV cache with probe-like content. Without this, the first real anchor call may produce different output due to GPU memory layout effects.
warmup_procedure.step_2: Wait 3 seconds for GPU state to stabilize.
warmup_procedure.step_3: Run all 5 anchor probes (B1-B5) with frozen parameters (temp=0, seed=42, top_k=1, max_tokens=2048).
warmup_procedure.step_4: Run the same 5 anchors a second time. If any score differs between step 3 and step 4, the session has not stabilized — repeat steps 1-4 up to 2 more times.
warmup_procedure.step_5: If step 4 scores match step 3, proceed to admission check.

reference_baseline.note: The design baseline from the initial warm session (commit 4b4b083). This is the reference, not the only valid operating point.

hard_fail_rules: Any anchor weakness_type label differs from reference → SESSION REJECTED. The model is generating materially different responses. Campaign measurements would not be comparable.
Mean absolute score deviation across 5 anchors > 0.05 → SESSION REJECTED. Systematic drift too large for delta=0.01 success threshold.
Any single anchor score deviation > 0.15 → SESSION REJECTED. Individual probe too far from reference to be interpretable.
soft_warn_rules: Any single anchor score deviation > 0.03 → LOG WARNING. Session is admitted but the affected anchor's measurements carry a noted drift caveat.
Mean absolute score deviation > 0.02 → LOG WARNING. Session is within tolerance but not pristine.
admission_check.if_rejected: Do not proceed with campaign work. Investigate cause (model file integrity, server flags, GPU state). If irrecoverable, establish a new reference baseline with full 5-run validation and document the baseline shift in the campaign log.
admission_check.if_admitted: Log the session admission result (timestamp, anchor scores, deviations, warnings). All campaign measurements in this session are valid until server restart.

non_anchor_sentinels.purpose: Anchor admission is necessary but not proven sufficient. Spot-check a fixed set of non-anchor probes to verify broader stability.
non_anchor_sentinels.rationale: One per campaign domain, chosen from fit pools (not holdout — holdout must remain untouched until holdout phase). Scores between 0.840-0.900, sensitive to drift.
non_anchor_sentinels.check: Run after anchor admission. Log scores. If any sentinel deviates > 0.10 from full_baseline.json reference, log as WARNING but do not reject session (anchors are the binding gate).

campaign_limitation.statement: The measurement instrument (llama-server + CUDA + KV cache) has session-dependent behavior. Cold-start drift up to 0.116 was observed on keyword-boundary probes. Evidence is valid within a controlled warm-session operating envelope, not across arbitrary server restarts.
campaign_limitation.containment: Single-session discipline with explicit admission gate.
campaign_limitation.post_campaign_target: Investigate and eliminate session-dependent inference variance. Possible approaches: deterministic CUDA mode, fixed KV cache initialization, model warm-start checkpoint.
